free_text: Elastic scattering of 24.7 MeV alpha particles
Tabulated data are given.
120 cm cyclotron was used.
The targets were prepared from spectroscopically or
chemically pure samples by means of vacuum
evaporation or rolling. The thickness of targets in
no case exceeded 2.3 mg/cm2.
Angular distributions were measured using a
semiconductor counter as a detector of alpha
particles. The intensity of the beam was monitored by
two semiconductor detectors, one viewing the target
at a fixed angle of about 30 degr., the second one
counting alpha particles scattered from a gold target
placed in the beam in front of the Faraday cup. The
energy resolution was 120 keV.
In order to obtain better accuracy in the
determination of the absolute values of the cross
sections several independent measurements were made
with a few different targets for each nuclide.
LV, SD.
SS+LV+On. Digitized Rutherford ratio data
were replaced by tabulated one from INP-347,1964
report. Differential cross section data were added.
On. Minor revision in 006.
SiO(2) target with 0.301 mg/cm2 thickness was used.
Error of absolute value of cross section.
